context: Monday June 15 — a risk-on relief day, but a bounce INTO a binary. The US-Iran 'Islamabad declaration' was signed Sunday Jun 14 and lifts the US naval blockade on the Strait of Hormuz (~20% of world oil); WTI fell ~3.2% to $84.88 and Brent dropped ~4% toward $83, easing energy-supply fears and lifting global risk assets. Crypto repriced hard: ~$246M of shorts were wiped, BTC printed a two-week high above $65,500 (now ~$65,936, +2.2% 24h / +3.95% 7d), and the whole basket is green — XRP $1.21 (+5.6%, strongest major), SUI $0.805 (+6.1%), HBAR $0.0818 (+3.8%). Fear & Greed sits at 20 (Extreme Fear) — still low, but recovering from 13 on Jun 13. The hot May CPI 'trapdoor' feared last week did NOT open: the 4.2% y/y headline (released Jun 10) was 60%+ energy-driven, core was benign at +0.2% m/m / 2.9% y/y, and the Iran deal has since reversed the very energy spike that drove the print. Positioning tells are turning less bearish too — Coinbase Premium -0.15% (vs deeply negative early June) and +$85.9M of BTC spot-ETF inflows on Jun 15 against the prior week's ~$1.72B of outflows. The single event that frames the rest of the week is the FOMC decision Wed Jun 17 (2:00 PM ET) — Kevin Warsh's FIRST meeting as Chair. A rate-HOLD is 97% priced (3.50-3.75%), so the binary is not the rate: it's the dot-plot/SEP and an expected explicit shift from an easing bias to a NEUTRAL stance. Trade plan today: DO NOT chase the green into the FOMC — size stays small, buy OTE pullbacks not breakouts. $66.5K is BTC's shelf to reclaim above, $63.7K (24h low) the first support, $60K still THE line; a hawkish dot-plot is the one thing that turns today's relief into a fade.

tickers.thesis: BTC printed a two-week high above $65,500 (now ~$65,936, +2.2% 24h) on the US-Iran 'Islamabad declaration' signed Sunday Jun 14 — the deal lifts the US naval blockade on the Strait of Hormuz (~20% of world oil), sending WTI -3.2% to ~$84.88 and Brent -4% toward $83, easing energy-supply fears and lifting risk assets. The relief move wiped out ~$246M of crypto shorts and pulled +$85.9M of spot-ETF inflows on the day. Crucially the energy spike that drove the hot May CPI headline (4.2% y/y, released Jun 10) has now REVERSED — and core CPI was already benign at +0.2% m/m / 2.9% y/y. But this is a relief bounce INTO a binary: the FOMC decision is tomorrow-plus (Jun 16-17), Warsh's first meeting as Chair, with the dot-plot/SEP the real risk (97% rate-HOLD priced; the move is an explicit shift from easing-bias to NEUTRAL). NEUTRAL, HIGH: don't chase green into the FOMC. $66.5K is the shelf to reclaim, $63.7K (24h low) the first support, $60K still THE line below.
levels: Cycle pivot / hard support ($59,100 = Jun-6 low)

setup.entry: $63,700 – $64,500  (OTE pullback — do NOT chase the two-week high into FOMC)
setup.stop: $62,800  (below the 24h-low demand; pads the $63K round number)
setup.tp1: $68,000  (R:R ~2.3R — take 50% at the pre-drop supply shelf)
setup.tp2: $70,000  (R:R ~3.6R — hold runner only on a dovish dot-plot)
setup.invalidation: Daily close below $62,000; or a hawkish Jun-17 dot-plot re-rates the curve

May CPI (Jun 10): headline 4.2% y/y HOT but energy-driven (60%+); core benign +0.2% m/m / 2.9% y/y
Spot ETF flows turning — +$85.9M Jun 15 vs ~$1.72B outflows the week ending Jun 5 (IBIT -$1.34B)

markov.sentence: Regime BULL (20-day +37.8%). Today (Mon Jun 15): model favours BULL 87% (signal +87.0%, long tilt). Day after (Tue Jun 16): BULL 77% — continuation.

tickers.thesis: XRP is the strongest major today, +5.6% to ~$1.21 — leading the risk-on relief on the Iran deal and reclaiming $1.18. The structural bid is intact and diverging from BTC: spot XRP ETFs sit at $1.43B cumulative (May record month $131.94M), with the seven US funds now holding 840M+ XRP (~1.5% of supply). Legislatively, CLARITY cleared Senate Banking 15-9 (May 14) and was placed on the Senate floor calendar Jun 1 — but leadership still has to schedule the floor vote and merge the Banking text with the Senate Ag version (Galaxy Digital pegs ~60% passage odds this year). So the catalyst is real but not imminent-this-week. NEUTRAL/HIGH: relative-strength name, but no momentum chase into the FOMC. Range $1.13-$1.40; $1.18 is the level that has to hold on a pullback, the OTE long into $1.16-$1.18 is the cleaner R:R.

insights.title: Sentiment flat: F&G 20 (Extreme Fear), Δ +2 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 20 (Extreme Fear) at 11:20Z — +2 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: Cross-exchange divergence on SUI: 0.62%
insights.body: Multi-source price triangulation (CoinGecko + Binance + Bybit) shows SUI divergence of 0.62% at 11:20Z. Divergence >0.5% across major venues is a thin-liquidity / arb-window signal. At 0.62% it's still in normal range but worth monitoring — widening = exit signal for sized trades on SUI.

insights.title: The Iran Deal Reversed the One Thing That Made CPI Look Scary
insights.body: Last week's binary read flipped. The May CPI headline (4.2% y/y, released Jun 10) looked like a trapdoor — but it was 60%+ energy (+3.9% m/m), while CORE was benign at +0.2% m/m / 2.9% y/y and core commodities actually fell 0.1% (muted tariffs). Then on Sunday Jun 14 the US-Iran 'Islamabad declaration' lifted the Hormuz blockade and sent WTI -3.2% / Brent -4%, directly reversing the energy spike that drove the hot headline. So the inflation scare that pressured the basket into early June has materially eased going into the FOMC. The relief is real — but it's a bounce into Wednesday's dot-plot, not an all-clear. Don't confuse the two.

insights.title: FOMC Wed Jun 17 Is the Binary — and It's the Dots, Not the Rate
insights.body: Warsh's first decision as Chair lands Wed 2:00 PM ET with a 97% rate-HOLD already priced (3.50-3.75%). The market-moving content is the Summary of Economic Projections (dot-plot) and an expected explicit shift from an easing bias to a NEUTRAL stance — 72 of 102 economists see no cuts before year-end. Trade implication: today's risk-on tape can run a little further on momentum, but the asymmetric risk is a hawkish set of dots that re-rates the whole curve and fades the bounce. Keep size small, buy pullbacks not breakouts, and don't carry oversized risk through 2:00 PM Wednesday. The reaction candle, not the relief, sets next week's direction.

insights.title: Positioning Tells Are Turning — De-Risking Is Easing, Not Reversing
insights.body: Two signals that were screaming 'institutional exit' in early June are softening. The Coinbase Premium Index, deeply negative through the early-June flush, sat at just -0.15% (Jun 2-3) and the Iran-deal repricing pulled +$85.9M of BTC spot-ETF inflows on Jun 15 against the prior week's ~$1.72B of outflows (IBIT -$1.34B). That's de-risking EASING, not a confirmed reversal — one green day after a 13-print Fear low isn't a trend. But it does mean the downside pressure that defined the last two weeks has lifted at the margin. Watch whether the ETF inflow holds for 2-3 sessions: that's the tell that the institutional bid is genuinely back, not just short-covering.

insights.title: XRP Is Leading — Relative Strength + CLARITY Optionality
insights.body: XRP is the strongest major today (+5.6% to ~$1.21) and the cleanest structural story in the basket. Spot XRP ETFs are at $1.43B cumulative (May record $131.94M month) with 7 funds now holding 840M+ XRP (~1.5% of supply) — flows that diverged from BTC's outflows through the worst of the selloff. CLARITY cleared Senate Banking 15-9 (May 14) and is on the Senate floor calendar (placed Jun 1), though leadership still must schedule the vote and merge the Banking/Ag texts (Galaxy ~60% passage odds this year). NEUTRAL/HIGH: this is the OTE-pullback name, not a momentum chase — buy $1.16-$1.18 on a dip, not $1.21 into the FOMC. A clean floor-vote schedule headline is the upside catalyst.

insights.title: SUI's Idiosyncratic Bid Is Building — WaterX Goes Full Platform
insights.body: SUI (+6.1% to ~$0.805, +7.0% on the week) has the best alt-specific story today. The structural-short overhang is gone (May outages resolved via the Jun 1 upgrade + post-mortem), and WaterX has launched its FULL platform on Sui — perpetuals, prediction markets, and an RWA/tokenized-stock engine including pre-IPO markets. Its first SpaceX pre-IPO quota sold out in ~50 minutes, a 2026-FIFA-World-Cup prediction market is planned, and network throughput hit 1,097 TPS on Jun 5. NEUTRAL/MED, range $0.745-$0.95: reclaim-and-hold $0.80 keeps the bounce alive toward $0.88, and the OTE long into $0.75-$0.77 is the structured entry. Re-add a SHORT only on a 4th outage.
